sources.sourceName: OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (answer key for odd-numbered review questions)

meta.notes: Psychology (PSBB) items scraped verbatim from OpenStax Psychology 2e Ch. 5 and Ch. 6 review-questions pages; correct answers from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), odd-numbered review questions only. Live OpenStax pages show CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 plus a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing/non-AI per PRD.

questions.stem: ________ refers to the minimum amount of stimulus energy required to be detected 50% of the time.
questions.explanation: The absolute threshold is the minimum stimulus energy needed for a person to detect a stimulus 50% of the time.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q1 = A.

questions.stem: ________ involves the conversion of sensory stimulus energy into neural impulses.
questions.explanation: Transduction is the conversion of physical sensory stimulus energy into the electrochemical neural impulses the nervous system can process.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q3 = D.

questions.stem: Which of the following correctly matches the pattern in our perception of color as we move from short wavelengths to long wavelengths?
questions.explanation: Wavelength increases from yellow (~570 nm) to orange (~600 nm) to red (~700 nm), so moving from shorter to longer wavelengths gives yellow to orange to red.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q5 = B.

questions.stem: The electromagnetic spectrum includes ________.
questions.explanation: The electromagnetic spectrum spans radio waves, infrared, visible light, ultraviolet, x-rays, and more, so all of the listed options are included.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q7 = D.

questions.stem: The quality of a sound that is affected by frequency, amplitude, and timing of the sound wave is known as ________.
questions.explanation: Timbre is the quality or 'color' of a sound that lets us distinguish sources; it depends on the combination of frequency, amplitude, and timing of the sound wave.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q9 = D.

questions.stem: ________ operate best under bright light conditions.
questions.explanation: Cones are the photoreceptors specialized for color and fine detail and operate best under bright (photopic) light; rods handle dim light.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q11 = A.

questions.stem: If you were to stare at a green dot for a relatively long period of time and then shift your gaze to a blank white screen, you would see a ________ negative afterimage.
questions.explanation: Opponent-process theory predicts that fatiguing the green channel produces a red negative afterimage, because red and green are opponent colors.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q13 = D.

questions.stem: The three ossicles of the middle ear are known as ________.
questions.explanation: The three middle-ear ossicles are the malleus, incus, and stapes, also known by their common names hammer, anvil, and stirrup, so both A and B are correct.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q15 = D.

questions.stem: Cues that require two ears are referred to as ________ cues.
questions.explanation: Binaural cues are localization cues that require both ears (for example, interaural time and intensity differences). Monaural cues require only one ear.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q17 = D.

questions.stem: Which taste is associated with monosodium glutamate?
questions.explanation: Monosodium glutamate (MSG) elicits the savory taste known as umami, one of the five basic tastes.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q19 = C.

questions.stem: Which of the following is involved in maintaining balance and body posture?
questions.explanation: The vestibular system of the inner ear senses head position and movement and is essential for balance and body posture.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q21 = D.

questions.stem: Our tendency to perceive things as complete objects rather than as a series of parts is known as the principle of ________.
questions.explanation: The Gestalt principle of closure is our tendency to fill in gaps and perceive incomplete figures as complete, whole objects.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q23 = A.

questions.stem: The main point of focus in a visual display is known as the ________.
questions.explanation: In figure-ground organization, the figure is the main object of focus that stands out from the background (ground).
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 5 Q25 = D.

questions.stem: Which of the following is an example of a reflex that occurs at some point in the development of a human being?
questions.explanation: A reflex is an unlearned, automatic response to a stimulus. An infant sucking on a nipple is a reflex, whereas the other options are learned behaviors.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 6 Q1 = C.

questions.stem: Two forms of associative learning are ________ and ________.
questions.explanation: The two major forms of associative learning are classical conditioning and operant conditioning. (Classical and Pavlovian conditioning are the same thing.)
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 6 Q3 = A.

questions.stem: A stimulus that does not initially elicit a response in an organism is a(n) ________.
questions.explanation: A neutral stimulus initially produces no relevant response; through classical conditioning it can become a conditioned stimulus.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 6 Q5 = B.

questions.stem: Extinction occurs when ________.

choices.label: A
choices.text: the conditioned stimulus is presented repeatedly without being paired with an unconditioned stimulus

choices.label: B
choices.text: the unconditioned stimulus is presented repeatedly without being paired with a conditioned stimulus

choices.label: C
choices.text: the neutral stimulus is presented repeatedly without being paired with an unconditioned stimulus

choices.label: D
choices.text: the neutral stimulus is presented repeatedly without being paired with a conditioned stimulus

questions.explanation: In classical conditioning, extinction is the weakening of the conditioned response when the conditioned stimulus is repeatedly presented without the unconditioned stimulus.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 6 Q7 = A.

questions.stem: ________ is when you take away a pleasant stimulus to stop a behavior.
questions.explanation: Negative punishment removes a pleasant stimulus to decrease a behavior (for example, taking away a privilege). 'Negative' means something is removed; 'punishment' means the behavior decreases.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 6 Q9 = D.

questions.stem: Rewarding successive approximations toward a target behavior is ________.
questions.explanation: Shaping is reinforcing successive approximations, that is, rewarding behaviors that get progressively closer to a desired target behavior.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 6 Q11 = A.

questions.stem: The person who performs a behavior that serves as an example is called a ________.
questions.explanation: In observational learning, the individual who performs the behavior that is observed and imitated is the model.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 6 Q13 = B.

questions.stem: Which is the correct order of steps in the modeling process?
questions.explanation: Bandura's four steps of observational learning (modeling) occur in the order attention, retention, reproduction, and motivation.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 6 Q15 = A.
